His dog was in the bed of the truck under a canopy, the doors were unlocked and his cell phone was on the front seat, but there was no sign of Damon. The APD Auxiliary Search Team, assisted by a dog trained to find cadavers, hunted for Bonds in the area around his truck but found no signs of him. Damon's friends and family insist that he never would have left his dog and are convinced he met with foul play.

Name: Hiroko Nemoto
Circumstances of Disappearance:
Hiroko Nemoto, a Japanese national studying English at Michigan State University, lived in East Lansing, Michigan when she traveled to Alaska. She had just finished the spring semester and only 10 credits away from earning her bachelor's degree. Nemoto told a friend she intended to stay in Alaska for a month, But she purchased only a one-way ticket from Northwest Airlines. 

Nemoto spent her first night in Alaska at the Denali Windsong Lodge. The next day, she traveled to Anchorage and spent the night at a hostel at 700 H Street. Nemoto had made reservations June 6 for the ferry from Whittier to Valdez and had a reservation at the Westmark Hotel in Valdez. Both tickets were used, but she never showed up either at the ferry or the hotel.
 
Local police tracked her to the Windbreak Hotel in Wasilla on June 8, 1998. 

Nemoto's mother, who lives in Ibaraki, contacted the Japanese Consulate in Michigan about a month after her daughter arrived in Alaska. The Japanese Consulate then called East Lansing police. Apparently Nemoto, a straight-A student, was depressed after receiving a B in a class.

Name: Laura Kuckes
Circumstances of Disappearance: On 07/27/1990, Laura and Isaac Kayutak (MP #66671) left Barrow on an outing that was to have lasted about 10 days. They were visited at their camp on 08/04/1990 by Barrow residents seeking directions to a fishing camp on the Chipp River. This was the last time they were seen. On 08/08/1990, searchers found the pair's 18 foot skiff overturned and stuck on a sandbar in Admiralty Bay, about 45 miles southeast of Barrow, the northernmost community in the United States. A sleeping bag and Laura's dog, which had drowned, were found as well. Searchers later found canvas bags containing camping gear and medication owned by the pair. It is presumed that Isaac and Laura were headed back to Barrow when their skiff capsized.

Name: Daniel John Novcaski
Circumstances of Disappearance: Daniel Novcaski was last seen on 09/09/1989 during a boating accident near St Lazaria Island by Sitka. He is missing with Daniel Jones (MP #67223). Per media, Daniel Jones' 13' Boston Whaler was found not long after the search for the two men began. The boat was anchored just offshore from St Lazaria and had food, gas, and some halibut on board. The two men are believed to have left the smaller boat there and continued fishing from Daniel Novcaski's larger 17' fiberglass shift. The two men did not return when expected and the wife of one of the men reported them missing. Both men are presumed to be deceased but their bodies have never been found.

Name: Ronald Dale Alspaugh
Circumstances of Disappearance: Ronald was last seen on 09/02/1989. Per media, he was canoeing with four other Nikiski residents from McKinley Village to Riley Creek in two canoes lashed together catamaran-style. The group reached Riley Creek late Saturday and was taking the canoes out of the river when they were swamped by a wave. The other individuals made it to shore but Ronald went back to the canoes to save equipment. He was last seen floating down the river with the canoes and he was going under. The canoes were found on Sunday wrapped around an abutment at Jonesville Bridge, the first bridge north of the entrance to Denali National Park. Ronald's body was never found.

Name: Andrea Mona Altiery
Circumstances of Disappearance:
Ms. Altiery was reported missing by her girlfriend on 12/2/1981. The claimant advised that Ms. Altiery worked as an exotic dancer in downtown Anchorage, and was last seen going to meet an unknown male for a photo shoot, and Ms. Altiery never returned home. She was last seen taking a Checker Cab to the Boniface Mall around 11:00pm in Anchorage, Alaska.

Ms. Altiery was also known by her stage name, “Enchantment” and by a nickname, “Fish”.

Ms. Altiery was described as a white, female adult with a medium complexion, between 23 to 25 years of age. She was reported as being between 5’4” to 5’6” tall, and between 115 to 125 pounds. Ms. Altiery had medium to long brown hair that she wore straightened, and had brown eyes. 

Ms. Altiery was last seen wearing a black leather jacket, red sweater, blue jeans, brown cowboy boots, and may have been carrying a small, flowered make-up bag and black zipper purse.

Ms. Altiery was also reported to have been wearing a golden-colored ring with one pearl, a golden-colored ring with two pearls, an antique wedding band set with a rose-set top, and a custom made golden necklace shaped like a salmon with a diamond set as the eye. 

Further investigation has revealed several pieces of Ms. Altiery’s jewelry was recovered from serial killer Robert C. Hansen’s home during a search warrant executed in 1983. It is believed that Ms. Altiery could be a victim of Robert C. Hansen.
Clothing Description:
Black leather jacket
Red sweater
Blue jeans

Name: Kaino A. Dixon
Circumstances of Disappearance: Media reports that Dwight Florin (MP #67237) and Kaino Dixon were crewmen on the fishing vessel, Cougar. They left the Cougar in a 12' aluminum fishing skiff, Dawn, late on Thursday, 11/12/1981 to hunt on Kodiak Island. The men apparently were stranded by increasingly bad weather which churned up to 6-8' breakers near the beach. As night fell, they flashed an SOS to the Cougar and later fired a distress flare. Then later, they fired a non-distress flare. Crewmen from the fishing vessel tried to reach the island Thursday night but were turned back by rough seas. When the crewmen finally reached shore on Friday, 11/13/1981, they found no sign of the men or the skiff. Both men are presumed deceased.

John told the climber that he had 14 days of food consisting of powdered milk, honey, sugar and flour, a bivi sack, snowmobile suit, no tent or sleeping bag, VB boots, a red plastic sled, snowshoes, blue pants, blue jacket, and approximately 20 wands for marking trails. Name: Roxane Easland
Circumstances of Disappearance:
Ms. Easland was reported missing by a friend on 7/2/1980 under the alias Karen Lee Baunsgard. The claimant advised that she and her friends had not heard from or seen Ms. Easland since the afternoon of 6/28/1980, when Ms. Easland was supposed to meet an unknown male on 4th Avenue in downtown Anchorage.
 
Ms. Easland had been staying at the Budget Motel on Spenard Road in Anchorage with her boyfriend for the previous two weeks.
 
Ms. Easland was described as a white female adult, between 23 to 25 years of age. She was reported as being between 5’2” to 5’4” tall, and between 90 to 105 pounds, with medium complexion, shoulder-length straight brown hair and brown eyes. She has a birth mark on the top of her right thigh, and a birth mark on the top of her shoulder. She was reported to occasionally wear wire-framed glasses.

Ms. Easland was last seen wearing a short, brown leather coat with a fur collar, pants, and black high heeled boots.

It is believed that Ms. Easland is a victim of serial killer Robert C. Hansen.
Scar/Mark: Birth mark on the top of right thigh; birth mark on the top of shoulder (unknown which)
Clothing Description:
Short, brown leather coat with dark fur collar
Light colored top
Jeans or slacks

Circumstances of Disappearance: Anthony was in a skiff and attempting to travel from Halibut Cove to Homer on 08/09/1973. His skiff was found overturned in China Poot Bay on 08/09/1973. He is presumed to have drowned.

Name: David Brown Allen
Circumstances of Disappearance: David departed from Eielson Bluffs in Denali National Park for a day hike to the Red Mountain area and never returned. Multiple searches of the area were unsuccessful in locating him.
Scar/Mark: Scar on the back of the head where no hair grew
Clothing Description:
Levis (changed into shorts later)
Off-white Wool sweater
Tan or dark green chamois shirt

Name: Megan Siobhan Emerick
Circumstances of Disappearance: Megan was last seen on July 7, 1973. Megan's front teeth protrude. She was last known to be wearing blue jeans, a white long sleeve checkered shirt, a brown short sleeve sweatshirt, and suede ski boots.
Clothing Description:
Blue jeans
Brown short sleeve sweatshirt
White long sleeve checkered shirt

Name: George Robert McClure
Circumstances of Disappearance: George was last seen on a raft on 05/18/1973 on Wild River, north of Koyukuk River.

Name: Charles Brennan
Circumstances of Disappearance: Charles Brennan fell off a tanker ladder anchored in Kachemak Bay.

Name: Mitchell Joseph Teevan
Circumstances of Disappearance: Mitchell routinely sent letters to his family from Nome, Alaska after moving there around 1965. May have also been living in the Fairbanks area. The letters stopped around 1973 and no one has heard from him since. Date of last contact is approximate.
Scar/Mark: Possible scar/mark on right hip (gunshot wound, 1958)

Name: Ephin Chulin
Circumstances of Disappearance: Last seen on 11/14/1972. Suffering from a medical condition.

Name: Marvin Edward Adair
Circumstances of Disappearance: Marvin Adiar was hunting near Placer River Valley near Portage and was separated from his hunting party. Despite search efforts, he was never located.
Scar/Mark: Scar on scalp; Burn scar on right forearm
Clothing Description:
Red or brown dacron insulated vest with name tag "Adair" sewn on right breast
Blue Levi jeans
Gold/brown wool Pendleton CPO wool shirt

Name: Don Edgar Jones
Circumstances of Disappearance: Missing aircraft, white and orange Cessna H1812H, overdue from Anchorage to Juneau. Also missing are Nicholas Begich, Thomas Boggs Sr and Russel Brown.

Name: Russel LeRoy Brown
Circumstances of Disappearance: Missing aircraft, white and orange Cessna N1812N overdue from Anchorage to Juneau. Also missing are Nicholas Begich, Thomas Boggs Sr, and Don Jones.

Name: Nicholas Joseph Begich
Circumstances of Disappearance: Missing aircraft, white and orange Cessna N1812H overdue from Anchorage to Juneau. Also missing are Thomas Boggs Sr, Russel Brown and Don Jones. Searches were unsuccessful in locating them.

Name: Thomas Hale Boggs
Circumstances of Disappearance: Thomas was last seen aboard an airplane that departed Anchorage, Alaska en route to Juneau, Alaska on October 16, 1972. During a campaign fundraising tour, Thomas Hale Boggs Sr. and fellow congressman, Nicholas Begich boarded a twin-engine Cessna 310 airplane FAA registration number N1812H. The plane disappeared in the Chugach Mountain Range in Southeast Alaska. Also aboard the plane were the pilot, Don Edgar Jonz and Congressman Begich’s aide, Russell L. Brown.

Name: John Peter Cheplak
Circumstances of Disappearance: Aboard F/V "Addington" which sank in the Shelikof Straight near Cape Uganik. Also missing is Melvin Torsen.

Name: Melvin William Torsen
Circumstances of Disappearance: Aboard F/V "Addington" which sank in the Shelikof Strait near Cape Uganik. Also missing is Clarence "John" Chepak.

Name: Douglas Lawrence Simmons
Circumstances of Disappearance: Douglas was lost from F/V Arlene Gale in Onion Bay near Raspberry Island near Kodiak on 04/27/1971.

Name: John Ronald Gould
Circumstances of Disappearance: Missing from 14' skiff while enroute from Belkofski to King Cove.

Name: Archie Olanna
Circumstances of Disappearance: Travelling via boat between Brevig Mission and Point Clarence with Fanny Olanna. Boat was found abandoned on a beach near Port Clarence Coast Guard Station.

Name: Fanny Olanna
Circumstances of Disappearance: Travelling via boat between Brevig Mission and Point Clarence with Archie Olanna. Boat was found abandoned on a beach near Port Clarence Coast Guard Station.

Name: Kenneth James Johnston
Circumstances of Disappearance: On 09/06/1967, Kenneth was on board a Cessna 172 (tail #N3789L) when it went missing enroute from Northway to Juneau.

Name: Delbert Dalton Hobbs
Circumstances of Disappearance: A friend reported that he last spoke to Delbert on the afternoon of 08/20/1966 and they had planned a trip for the following morning. Sometime that night, Delbert left his trailer and the trailer was padlocked from the outside. His vehicles were still parked at his residence.

Name: Dennis S Pungowiyi
Circumstances of Disappearance: Dennis Pungowiyi and his hunting partner's boat was found swamped near a cabin in Southeast Cape, Saint Lawrence Island.

Name: William Joseph Parsons Jr.
Circumstances of Disappearance: William Parsons Jr. was last seen at his residence after a serious argument in approximately August 1965.

Name: Vern Stephen Pratt
Circumstances of Disappearance: Vern was involved in a hit and run accident in Anchorage on the evening of 11/06/1961. A witness reported that Vern was seen in his vehicle in the ditch near the Portage Bar on 11/06/1961 between 10:00 and 10:30 p.m. on 11/06/1961. He appeared to be intoxicated and laid down in the front seat of his vehicle and apparently went to sleep. At approximately 9:00 a.m. on 11/07/1961, the witness saw the vehicle still in the ditch, but Vern was no longer in it.
Eye Description: Extreme myopia; could not see without his glasses
Scar/Mark:
1 inch scar on right wrist
1 inch scar on left arm; Brown mark on torso (birthmark?) left side
Clothing Description:
Charcoal colored slacks
White shirt
Light gray wool jacket with knit collar and knit cuffs

Name: Tuck Esenituk
Circumstances of Disappearance: Tuck was last known to be traveling from Brevig Mission to Teller.

Name: Harry Dick Pitka
Circumstances of Disappearance: Harry Pitka was last seen traveling via boat from Nulato to Ruby. Also missing with him are Claude Demoski, Bertha Demoski, Leo Demoski and Emmett Nollner.

Name: Leo Demoski, Jr.
Circumstances of Disappearance: Leo Demoski was last seen traveling via boat from Nulato to Ruby. Also missing with him are Emmett Nollner, Bertha Demoski, Claude Demoski and Harry Pitka.

Name: Emmett Nollner
Circumstances of Disappearance: Emmett Nollner was last seen traveling via boat from Nulato to Ruby. Also missing with him are Claude Demoski, Bertha Demoski, Leo Demoski and Harry Pitka.

Name: Bertha Genieve Demoski
Circumstances of Disappearance: Bertha Demoski was last seen traveling via boat from Nulato to Ruby. Also missing with her are Claude Demoski, Emmett Nollner, Leo Demoski and Harry Pitka.

Name: Claude Alphons Demoski
Circumstances of Disappearance: Claude Demoski was last seen traveling via boat from Nulato to Ruby. Also missing with him are Bertha Demoski, Leo Demoski, Harry Pitka and Emmett Nollner.

Name: Donald George Macvicar
Circumstances of Disappearance: Donald was involved in a boating accident In Chandler Lake and is presumed to have drowned.

Name: George Alfred Tonsgard
Circumstances of Disappearance: George Tonsgard disappeared while scuba diving in the Gastineau Channel.
